addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1685	/* The block is the half-open command range [lstart, lend) of the LZ77 store. */
1686	__CPROVER_requires(lstart <= lend)
1687	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77, sizeof(*lz77)))
1688	__CPROVER_requires(lend <= lz77->size)
1689	/* The non-empty case reads pos[lstart], pos[lend-1], dists[lend-1] and
1690	   litlens[lend-1]; since lstart < lend <= size, all indices lie in [0, size).
1691	   These per-command buffers are the natural store-sized arrays.  The byte size
1692	   of the (8-byte-element) pos array must not wrap around when computed. */
1693	__CPROVER_requires(
1694	    lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->pos) >= lz77->size)
1695	__CPROVER_requires(
1696	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->pos, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->pos)))
1697	__CPROVER_requires(
1698	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->dists, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->dists)))
1699	__CPROVER_requires(
1700	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->litlens, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->litlens)))
1701	/* Pure read-only query: it modifies nothing. */
1702	__CPROVER_assigns()
1703	/* An empty command range spans zero bytes. */
1704	__CPROVER_ensures((lstart == lend) ==> (__CPROVER_return_value == 0))
1705	{
1706	    size_t l = lend - 1;
1707	    if (lstart == lend)
1708	        return 0;
1709	    return lz77->pos[l] + ((lz77->dists[l] == 0) ? 1 : lz77->litlens[l]) - lz77->pos[lstart];
1710	}

79	        failed_step (CbmcStep | None): The pipeline step that failed, or None on success.
80	        timed_out (bool): True iff any step hit the per-attempt timeout.

106

108	        and the return code is 0.

181	def run_cbmc(
182	    function_to_verify: str,
183	    file_containing_function_to_verify: str,
184	    include_dirs: list[str] | None = None,
185	    call_graph: CallGraph | None = None,
186	    cwd: str | None = None,
187	) -> RunCbmcResult:
188	    """Run CBMC on the given function with loop unwinding = 5, depth = 100.
189	
190	    The pipeline is split into three logical steps — `goto-cc`, `goto-instrument`, and
191	    `cbmc` — each run as its own subprocess so that failures can be attributed to a
192	    specific step. Up to three pipeline attempts are made: an initial run, a retry
193	    when an apparent recursive-inlining error is detected, and a retry when a missing
194	    callee body is detected. The timeout is per CBMC subprocess attempt, not a total
195	    budget; a timeout in any single step terminates the whole call.
196	
197	    Args:
198	        function_to_verify (str): Name of the function to verify.
199	        file_containing_function_to_verify (str): Path to the C file defining the function.
200	        include_dirs (list[str] | None): Directories to add to the C preprocessor's include
201	            search path. Forwarded to `goto-cc` as `-I` flags.
202	        call_graph (CallGraph | None): When provided, this pre-built call graph is used instead
203	            of parsing `file_containing_function_to_verify`.
204	        cwd (str | None): Working directory for every subprocess in the pipeline. The pipeline
205	            writes its intermediate `<function>.goto` / `checking-<function>-contracts.goto` files
206	            relative to this directory, so concurrent runs of the same function must each pass a
207	            distinct `cwd` to avoid clobbering one another. When None, subprocesses inherit the
208	            current working directory.
209	
210	    Returns:
211	        RunCbmcResult: The outcome of the run, naming the failed step (if any) and carrying
212	            a printable response and the relevant exit code.
213	    """
214	    if call_graph is None:
215	        path_to_raw_call_graph = construct_call_graph(file_containing_function_to_verify)
216	        call_graph = CallGraph(json.loads(Path(path_to_raw_call_graph).read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
217	    callees = get_in_file_callees_for(function_to_verify, call_graph)
218	    # Building the stub index is inexpensive for now (there is a single file).
219	    # Re-visit this if/when we have more stub files to parse.
220	    stub_index = build_stub_index()
221	    stub_paths = get_stub_paths_for(function_to_verify, call_graph, stub_index)
222	    nondet_callees = get_unstubbed_external_callees_for(function_to_verify, call_graph, stub_index)
223	
224	    step_records: list[dict] = []
225	
226	    # Initial attempt.
227	    result, combined_stdout, combined_stderr = _run_pipeline(
228	        function_to_verify,
229	        callees,
230	        file_containing_function_to_verify,
231	        stub_paths=stub_paths,
232	        include_dirs=include_dirs,
233	        prevent_macro_expansion=False,
234	        step_records=step_records,
235	        cwd=cwd,
236	    )
237	    # First, check if the run was successful or if it timed out.
238	    if result.cbmc_ran_successfully or result.timed_out:
239	        _log_invocation(file_containing_function_to_verify, result, step_records, nondet_callees)
240	        return result
241	
242	    # Recursion-inlining retry.
243	    if has_recursion_inlining_error_message(function_to_verify, combined_stdout, combined_stderr):
244	        callees = get_in_file_callees_for(
245	            function_to_verify,
246	            call_graph,
247	            include_self=call_graph.is_self_recursive(function_to_verify),
248	        )
249	        result, combined_stdout, combined_stderr = _run_pipeline(
250	            function_to_verify,
251	            callees,
252	            file_containing_function_to_verify,
253	            stub_paths=stub_paths,
254	            include_dirs=include_dirs,
255	            prevent_macro_expansion=False,
256	            step_records=step_records,
257	            cwd=cwd,
258	        )
259	
260	    # Missing-body retry: re-run with macro expansion suppressed.
261	    if not result.cbmc_ran_successfully and has_missing_body_for_callee_or_function_message(
262	        combined_stdout, combined_stderr
263	    ):
264	        result, combined_stdout, combined_stderr = _run_pipeline(
265	            function_to_verify,
266	            callees,
267	            file_containing_function_to_verify,
268	            stub_paths=stub_paths,
269	            include_dirs=include_dirs,
270	            prevent_macro_expansion=True,
271	            step_records=step_records,
272	            cwd=cwd,
273	        )
274	
275	    _log_invocation(file_containing_function_to_verify, result, step_records, nondet_callees)
276	    return result

279	def _run_pipeline(
280	    function_to_verify: str,
281	    callees: list[str],
282	    file_containing_function: str,
283	    stub_paths: list[str] | None,
284	    include_dirs: list[str] | None,
285	    prevent_macro_expansion: bool,
286	    step_records: list[dict],
287	    cwd: str | None = None,
288	) -> tuple[RunCbmcResult, str, str]:
289	    """Run the goto-cc → goto-instrument → cbmc pipeline once.
290	
291	    Each subprocess is run separately so the first failure (or timeout) can be attributed
292	    to its logical step. Step records are appended to `step_records` for the JSONL log.
293	
294	    Args:
295	        function_to_verify (str): The function under verification.
296	        callees (list[str]): Callees of the function, used by `--replace-call-with-contract`.
297	        file_containing_function (str): The C source file containing the function.
298	        stub_paths (list[str] | None): Extra `.c` stub files compiled in alongside the source.
299	        include_dirs (list[str] | None): Directories forwarded to `goto-cc` as `-I` flags.
300	        prevent_macro_expansion (bool): When True, disable macros CBMC can't model and inject
301	            the bundled C-library models before contract enforcement.
302	        step_records (list[dict]): Mutated in place — one record per subprocess invocation
303	            appended in order. Used by `_log_invocation` to produce the JSONL row.
304	        cwd (str | None): Working directory for every subprocess, forwarded to `_run_step`. The
305	            pipeline's intermediate `.goto` files are written relative to this directory.
306	
307	    Returns:
308	        tuple[RunCbmcResult, str, str]: The result of the pipeline plus the concatenated
309	            stdout and stderr across every step that ran. The concatenated output is used by
310	            the retry triggers in `run_cbmc`.
311	    """
312	    commands: list[tuple[CbmcStep, str]] = [
313	        (
314	            CbmcStep.GOTO_CC,
315	            _get_goto_cc_command(
316	                function_to_verify,
317	                file_containing_function,
318	                stub_paths=stub_paths,
319	                include_dirs=include_dirs,
320	                prevent_macro_expansion=prevent_macro_expansion,
321	            ),
322	        ),
323	    ]
324	    if prevent_macro_expansion:
325	        commands.append(
326	            (
327	                CbmcStep.GOTO_INSTRUMENT,
328	                _get_goto_instrument_add_library_command(function_to_verify),
329	            )
330	        )
331	    commands.extend(
332	        [
333	            (
334	                CbmcStep.GOTO_INSTRUMENT,
335	                _get_goto_instrument_unwind_command(function_to_verify),
336	            ),
337	            (
338	                CbmcStep.GOTO_INSTRUMENT,
339	                _get_goto_instrument_contract_command(function_to_verify, callees),
340	            ),
341	            (CbmcStep.CBMC, _get_cbmc_check_command(function_to_verify)),
342	        ]
343	    )
344	
345	    combined_stdout = ""
346	    combined_stderr = ""
347	    for step, command in commands:
348	        step_run = _run_step(step, command, cwd=cwd)
349	        step_records.append(
350	            {
351	                "step": step.value,
352	                "command": step_run.command,
353	                "returncode": step_run.returncode,
354	            }
355	        )
356	        combined_stdout += step_run.stdout
357	        combined_stderr += step_run.stderr
358	        if not step_run.succeeded:
359	            return (
360	                _result_from_failure(
361	                    function_to_verify, step_run, combined_stdout, combined_stderr
362	                ),
363	                combined_stdout,
364	                combined_stderr,
365	            )
366	
367	    return (
368	        RunCbmcResult(
369	            function=function_to_verify,
370	            failed_step=None,
371	            timed_out=False,
372	            returncode=0,
373	            response=f"{function_to_verify} verified successfully",
374	        ),
375	        combined_stdout,
376	        combined_stderr,
377	    )
378	
379	
380	def _run_step(step: CbmcStep, command: str, cwd: str | None = None) -> _StepRun:
381	    """Run one pipeline step as a subprocess with the per-step timeout.
382	
383	    Args:
384	        step (CbmcStep): The logical step this subprocess belongs to.
385	        command (str): The shell command to run.
386	        cwd (str | None): Working directory for the subprocess. When None, the subprocess
387	            inherits the current working directory. Distinct directories let concurrent
388	            pipelines for the same function avoid clobbering each other's `.goto` files.
389	
390	    Returns:
391	        _StepRun: The captured outcome, including stdout/stderr or the timeout sentinel.
392	    """
393	    try:
394	        completed = subprocess.run(
395	            command,
396	            capture_output=True,
397	            text=True,
398	            shell=True,
399	            check=False,
400	            timeout=_DEFAULT_RUN_CBMC_TIMEOUT_SEC,
401	            cwd=cwd,
402	        )
403	    except TimeoutExpired:
404	        return _StepRun(
405	            step=step,
406	            command=command,
407	            returncode=_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE,
408	            stdout="",
409	            stderr="",
410	            timed_out=True,
411	        )
412	    return _StepRun(
413	        step=step,
414	        command=command,
415	        returncode=completed.returncode,
416	        stdout=completed.stdout,
417	        stderr=completed.stderr,
418	        timed_out=False,
419	    )
420	
421	
422	def _result_from_failure(
423	    function: str, step_run: _StepRun, combined_stdout: str, combined_stderr: str
424	) -> RunCbmcResult:
425	    """Build a `RunCbmcResult` for a pipeline that failed at `step_run`.
426	
427	    Args:
428	        function (str): The function under verification.
429	        step_run (_StepRun): The step that failed or timed out.
430	        combined_stdout (str): Concatenated stdout across every step that ran (including
431	            the failing one). Used to render the failure response.
432	        combined_stderr (str): Concatenated stderr across every step that ran.
433	
434	    Returns:
435	        RunCbmcResult: A failure result whose `response` is a formatted, truncated block.
436	    """
437	    if step_run.timed_out:
438	        response = (
439	            f"Verification for '{function}' timed out after "
440	            f"{_DEFAULT_RUN_CBMC_TIMEOUT_SEC} second(s) during {step_run.step.value}"
441	        )
442	        return RunCbmcResult(
443	            function=function,
444	            failed_step=step_run.step,
445	            timed_out=True,
446	            returncode=_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE,
447	            response=response,
448	        )
449	    response = _format_failure_response(function, step_run.step, combined_stdout, combined_stderr)
450	    return RunCbmcResult(
451	        function=function,
452	        failed_step=step_run.step,
453	        timed_out=False,
454	        returncode=step_run.returncode,
455	        response=response,
456	    )
457	
458	
459	def has_recursion_inlining_error_message(function: str, stdout: str, stderr: str) -> bool:
460	    """Return True iff CBMC reports an error with inlining given function, which might be recursive.
461	
462	    Args:
463	        function (str): The function that might be recursive.
464	        stdout (str): The stdout of a CBMC command.
465	        stderr (str): The stderr of a CBMC command.
466	
467	    Returns:
468	        bool: True iff CBMC reports an error with inlining given function, which might be recursive.
469	    """
470	    recursion_error_message = f"Recursive call to '{function}' during inlining"
471	    return recursion_error_message in stdout or recursion_error_message in stderr
472	
473	
474	def has_missing_body_for_callee_or_function_message(stdout: str, stderr: str) -> bool:
475	    """Return True iff CBMC output indicates a callee or function body is missing.
476	
477	    The CBMC error output contains the string "no body for callee" when a callee of a function under
478	    verification is missing its body.
479	
480	    It contains the string "no body for function" in cases where a function body may be missing
481	    (e.g., when a stub file is not passed in).
482	
483	    In this case, it doesn't hurt to re-run CBMC while suppressing
484	    macro expansion (e.g., `isspace` in ctype.h expands to `__ctype_loc`, which CBMC lacks a model
485	    for).
486	
487	    Args:
488	        stdout (str): The stdout of a CBMC command.
489	        stderr (str): The stderr of a CBMC command.
490	
491	    Returns:
492	        bool: True iff CBMC output indicates a callee body is missing.
493	    """
494	    missing_callee_indicator = "no body for callee"
495	    missing_function_indicator = "no body for function"
496	    return (
497	        missing_callee_indicator in stdout
498	        or missing_callee_indicator in stderr
499	        or missing_function_indicator in stdout
500	        or missing_function_indicator in stderr
501	    )
502	
503	
504	def _format_failure_response(function: str, failed_step: CbmcStep, stdout: str, stderr: str) -> str:
505	    """Format a CBMC failure response, leading with the FAILURE lines.
506	
507	    After the header, the response always begins with the FAILURE lines extracted from
508	    stdout, so the verdict survives the harness's head-only inline preview: CBMC prints its
509	    FAILURE lines and verdict at the *tail* of stdout, which a positional truncation would
510	    otherwise bury. The full stderr and stdout follow; when the combined output exceeds
511	    `_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS`, each stream is replaced by its tail behind a truncation marker.
512	    For `goto-cc`/`goto-instrument` failures stdout has no FAILURE lines, so the response
513	    leads with stderr, where those tools print their diagnostics.
514	
515	    Args:
516	        function (str): The name of the function that failed verification.
517	        failed_step (CbmcStep): The pipeline step that failed.
518	        stdout (str): The concatenated stdout across every step that ran.
519	        stderr (str): The concatenated stderr across every step that ran.
520	
521	    Returns:
522	        str: The formatted CBMC failure response, leading with the FAILURE lines and
523	            truncated iff it has exceeded the char budget.
524	    """
525	    if failed_step is CbmcStep.CBMC:
526	        header = f"{function} failed to verify with the following errors:\n\n"
527	    else:
528	        header = f"{function} failed at {failed_step.value} with the following errors:\n\n"
529	
530	    failure_section = _failure_lines_section(stdout)
531	
532	    # Lead with the FAILURE lines, then show both streams in full when everything fits.
533	    full = f"{header}{failure_section}--- stderr ---\n{stderr}\n--- stdout ---\n{stdout}"
534	    if len(full) <= _MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS:
535	        return full
536	
537	    # Over budget: keep the leading FAILURE lines and replace each stream with its tail.
538	    # The `_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS`-wide placeholder pads the marker's digit count so the
539	    # actual marker (with the real dropped count) cannot push us over budget.
540	    digit_pad = str(_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS)
541	    marker = f"[... {digit_pad} characters truncated ...]\n"
542	    fixed = (
543	        f"{header}{failure_section}--- stderr (tail) ---\n{marker}--- stdout (tail) ---\n{marker}"
544	    )
545	    remaining = max(_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS - len(fixed), 0)
546	    stdout_budget = int(remaining * _STDOUT_TAIL_SHARE)
547	    stderr_budget = remaining - stdout_budget
548	
549	    stderr_section = _tail_section("stderr (tail)", stderr, stderr_budget)
550	    stdout_section = _tail_section("stdout (tail)", stdout, stdout_budget)
551	
552	    response = f"{header}{failure_section}{stderr_section}{stdout_section}"
553	    # Hard clamp: the per-section budget accounting can drift by a few chars
554	    # against the `fixed` estimate, so guarantee we never exceed the cap.
555	    return response[:_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS]
556	
557	
558	def _failure_lines_section(stdout: str) -> str:
559	    """Render the leading "FAILURE lines" section of a failure response, or "" if none.
560	
561	    Extracts the FAILURE lines from `stdout` and renders them as a labeled section, capped
562	    at half the total budget so a pathological run with tens of thousands of FAILURE lines
563	    can't blow past the limit on its own. Returns the empty string when stdout has no
564	    FAILURE lines (e.g. a goto-cc compile failure), so callers naturally lead with stderr.
565	
566	    Args:
567	        stdout (str): The concatenated stdout across every step that ran.
568	
569	    Returns:
570	        str: The labeled FAILURE-lines section ending in a newline, or "" if there are none.
571	    """
572	    failure_lines = [line for line in stdout.split("\n") if "FAILURE" in line]
573	    if not failure_lines:
574	        return ""
575	    failure_block = "\n".join(failure_lines)
576	    failure_cap = _MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS // 2
577	    if len(failure_block) > failure_cap:
578	        dropped = len(failure_block) - failure_cap
579	        failure_block = (
580	            f"[... {dropped} characters of FAILURE lines truncated ...]\n"
581	            f"{failure_block[-failure_cap:]}"
582	        )
583	    return f"--- FAILURE lines ---\n{failure_block}\n"
584	
585	
586	def _tail_section(label: str, content: str, budget: int) -> str:
587	    """Render a labeled section containing the tail of content within budget chars.
588	
589	    Args:
590	        label (str): The label of the section.
591	        content (str): The content to truncate.
592	        budget (int): The maximum number of characters to include in the section.
593	
594	    Returns:
595	        str: The labeled section containing the tail of content within budget chars.
596	    """
597	    if len(content) <= budget:
598	        body = content
599	    else:
600	        dropped = len(content) - budget
601	        body = f"[... {dropped} characters truncated ...]\n{content[-budget:]}"
602	    return f"--- {label} ---\n{body}\n"
603	
604	
605	def _log_invocation(
606	    file_under_verification: str,
607	    result: RunCbmcResult,
608	    step_records: list[dict],
609	    nondet_callees: list[str],
610	) -> None:
611	    """Log a CBMC invocation with the given arguments.
612	
613	    Args:
614	        file_under_verification (str): The file that contains the function under verification.
615	        result (RunCbmcResult): The final outcome of the `run_cbmc` call.
616	        step_records (list[dict]): Per-subprocess records (one entry per step invocation across
617	            every pipeline attempt), each with keys `step`, `command`, `returncode`.
618	        nondet_callees (list[str]): The list of callees that CBMC treated as non-deterministic
619	            during verification.
620	    """
621	    source_path = Path(file_under_verification)
622	    log_path = source_path.with_name(f"{source_path.stem}-cbmc-runs.jsonl")
623	    record = {
624	        "ts": datetime.now(UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
625	        "function": result.function,
626	        "file": file_under_verification,
627	        "failed_step": result.failed_step.value if result.failed_step is not None else None,
628	        "returncode": result.returncode,
629	        "timed_out": result.timed_out,
630	        "steps": step_records,
631	        "nondet_callees": nondet_callees,
632	    }
633	    try:
634	        with log_path.open("a") as f:
635	            f.write(json.dumps(record) + "\n")
636	    except OSError:
637	        # This does fail silently, but it shouldn't stop the tool from making progress.
638	        pass
639	
640	
641	def _get_goto_cc_command(
642	    function: str,
643	    file_containing_function: str,
644	    stub_paths: list[str] | None = None,
645	    include_dirs: list[str] | None = None,
646	    prevent_macro_expansion: bool = False,
647	) -> str:
648	    """Return the goto-cc command that compiles `file_containing_function` to a goto-binary.
649	
650	    This is the first step of the CBMC pipeline, exposed as a standalone helper so
651	    callers can run only the compile phase — useful for cheaply detecting inputs that goto-cc
652	    rejects (e.g., a body-mutated source where mutating `p - q` to `p + q` is illegal C).
653	
654	    Args:
655	        function (str): The function used as the goto-binary entry point.
656	        file_containing_function (str): Path to the C source file.
657	        stub_paths (list[str] | None): Extra `.c` stub files compiled in alongside the source.
658	        include_dirs (list[str] | None): Directories forwarded as `-I` flags.
659	        prevent_macro_expansion (bool): If True, disable macros that CBMC cannot model.
660	
661	    Returns:
662	        str: A single shell command suitable for `subprocess.run(..., shell=True)`.
663	    """
664	    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
665	    flags_disabling_macro_expansion = (
666	        f"{' '.join(_DISABLE_MACRO_FLAGS)} " if prevent_macro_expansion else ""
667	    )
668	    extra_stub_args = f" {' '.join(shlex.quote(p) for p in stub_paths)}" if stub_paths else ""
669	    include_flags = "".join(f" -I {shlex.quote(d)}" for d in include_dirs) if include_dirs else ""
670	    return (
671	        f"goto-cc {flags_disabling_macro_expansion}-o {quoted_function}.goto"
672	        f"{include_flags} "
673	        f"{shlex.quote(file_containing_function)}{extra_stub_args} "
674	        f"--function {quoted_function}"
675	    )
676	
677	
678	def _get_goto_instrument_add_library_command(function: str) -> str:
679	    """Return the `goto-instrument --add-library` command for `function`'s goto-binary.
680	
681	    This step injects CBMC's bundled C-library models (`isspace`, `strchr`, etc.) into
682	    the goto-binary before contract enforcement runs. It's only used when macro expansion
683	    is suppressed; without it, those calls are treated as nondeterministic, which loosens
684	    precision and can cause `goto-instrument` to emit "no body for function" warnings.
685	
686	    Args:
687	        function (str): The function whose goto-binary should receive the library models.
688	
689	    Returns:
690	        str: A single shell command.
691	    """
692	    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
693	    return f"goto-instrument --add-library {quoted_function}.goto {quoted_function}.goto"
694	
695	
696	def _get_goto_instrument_unwind_command(function: str) -> str:
697	    """Return the `goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind _CBMC_UNWIND` command for `function`.
698	
699	    Args:
700	        function (str): The function whose goto-binary should be unwound.
701	
702	    Returns:
703	        str: A single shell command.
704	    """
705	    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
706	    return (
707	        f"goto-instrument --partial-loops "
708	        f"--unwind {_CBMC_UNWIND} {quoted_function}.goto {quoted_function}.goto"
709	    )
710	
711	
712	def _get_goto_instrument_contract_command(function: str, callees: list[str]) -> str:
713	    """Return the `goto-instrument --enforce-contract` command for `function`.
714	
715	    Args:
716	        function (str): The function whose contract should be enforced.
717	        callees (list[str]): Callees whose contracts should be substituted for the call sites
718	            via `--replace-call-with-contract`.
719	
720	    Returns:
721	        str: A single shell command.
722	    """
723	    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
724	    replace_calls = "".join(f" --replace-call-with-contract {shlex.quote(c)}" for c in callees)
725	    return (
726	        f"goto-instrument{replace_calls} "
727	        f"--enforce-contract {quoted_function} "
728	        f"{quoted_function}.goto "
729	        f"checking-{quoted_function}-contracts.goto"
730	    )
731	
732	
733	def _get_cbmc_check_command(function: str) -> str:
734	    """Return the final `cbmc` command that checks `function`'s instrumented goto-binary.
735	
736	    Args:
737	        function (str): The function under verification.
738	
739	    Returns:
740	        str: A single shell command.
741	    """
742	    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
743	    return (
744	        f"cbmc checking-{quoted_function}-contracts.goto "
745	        f"--function {quoted_function} --depth {_CBMC_DEPTH}"
746	    )
747	
748	
749	def compile_with_goto_cc(
750	    function: str,
751	    file_path: str,
752	    include_dirs: list[str] | None = None,
753	) -> int:
754	    """Run only the goto-cc compile step on a C file and return its exit code.
755	
756	    A zero exit code means goto-cc accepted the input; non-zero means it rejected it.
757	
758	    Args:
759	        function (str): The function used as the goto-binary entry point.
760	        file_path (str): Path to the C file to compile.
761	        include_dirs (list[str] | None): Directories forwarded as `-I` flags.
762	
763	    Returns:
764	        int: goto-cc's exit code (0 == compiled successfully).
765	    """
766	    compilation_command = _get_goto_cc_command(function, file_path, include_dirs=include_dirs)
767	    try:
768	        result = subprocess.run(
769	            compilation_command,
770	            capture_output=True,
771	            text=True,
772	            shell=True,
773	            check=False,
774	            timeout=_GOTO_CC_TIMEOUT_SEC,
775	        )
776	    except TimeoutExpired:
777	        return _TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE
778	    return result.returncode
779	
780	
781	if __name__ == "__main__":
782	    main()

"""Run CBMC on a function.

Usage:
    % run-cbmc --function <FUNCTION_NAME> \
                       --file <PATH_TO_C_FILE> \
                       [-I <PATH_TO_INCLUDE_DIR(S)>]...
"""

import argparse
import json
import shlex
import subprocess
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from enum import StrEnum
from pathlib import Path
from subprocess import TimeoutExpired

from tools.construct_call_graph import construct_call_graph
from tools.util import (
    build_stub_index,
    get_in_file_callees_for,
    get_stub_paths_for,
    get_unstubbed_external_callees_for,
)
from tools.util.callgraph import CallGraph

# Char budget for failure responses. The harness persists full tool output to a file but
# previews only the head inline, so we keep responses bounded (a pathological CBMC run can
# emit hundreds of MB) and lead with the FAILURE lines (see `_format_failure_response`) so
# the verdict survives that head-only preview rather than being buried in the stdout tail.
_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS = 100_000

# Of the budget left after the header, the leading FAILURE lines, and section labels, this
# fraction is given to the stdout tail; the remainder goes to the stderr tail.
_STDOUT_TAIL_SHARE = 0.7

_DISABLE_MACRO_FLAGS = [
    "-D__NO_CTYPE"  # for ctype.h
]

# 10-minute timeout.
_DEFAULT_RUN_CBMC_TIMEOUT_SEC = 600

# goto-cc is fast; if it hasn't finished in this many seconds, the input is almost
# certainly pathological and should be treated as uncompilable.
_GOTO_CC_TIMEOUT_SEC = 30

# GNU `timeout(1)` convention: distinguishes a timeout from a CBMC verification failure
# both in the CLI exit code and in the JSONL invocation log.
_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE = 124

# Fixed values for CBMC's `--depth` and `--unwind` flags.
_CBMC_UNWIND = 5
_CBMC_DEPTH = 200


class CbmcStep(StrEnum):
    """Logical step in the CBMC verification pipeline.

    `run_cbmc` runs the pipeline as three logical steps; each maps to one or more
    underlying subprocess invocations. When a step fails, the result names the step
    so callers can distinguish e.g. a goto-cc compile rejection from a cbmc
    verification failure.
    """

    GOTO_CC = "goto-cc"
    GOTO_INSTRUMENT = "goto-instrument"
    CBMC = "cbmc"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunCbmcResult:
    """The outcome of running CBMC on a function.

    Attributes:
        function (str): The function under verification.
        failed_step (CbmcStep | None): The pipeline step that failed, or None on success.
        timed_out (bool): True iff any step hit the per-attempt timeout.
        returncode (int): 0 on success, `_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE` on timeout, otherwise the
            exit code of the failing subprocess (cbmc's verification exit code when
            `failed_step` is CBMC).
        response (str): Printable summary — a success line or a truncated failure block.
    """

    function: str
    failed_step: CbmcStep | None
    timed_out: bool
    returncode: int
    response: str

    @property
    def cbmc_ran_successfully(self) -> bool:
        """True iff the full pipeline ran without any step failing or timing out.

        Note: This is *not* equivalent to a successful verification result. See
        `is_function_verified`.

        """
        return self.failed_step is None and not self.timed_out

    @property
    def is_function_verified(self) -> bool:
        """True iff the function for this verification run is successfully verified.

        A function is successfully verified if all components of the CBMC pipeline ran successfully
        and the return code is 0.
        """
        return self.cbmc_ran_successfully and self.returncode == 0

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        """Return the string representation of this result, used for logging.

        Returns:
            str: The string representation of this result, used for logging.
        """
        if failed_step := self.failed_step:
            return f"{failed_step.value.upper()}_FAILED"
        if self.timed_out:
            return "TIMED_OUT"
        return "PASS" if self.is_function_verified else "FAIL"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class _StepRun:
    """Outcome of running one subprocess step.

    Attributes:
        step (CbmcStep): The logical step in the CBMC verification pipeline this subprocess belongs
            to.
        command (str): The shell command that was run.
        returncode (int): The subprocess exit code, or `_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE` on timeout.
        stdout (str): Captured stdout (empty on timeout).
        stderr (str): Captured stderr (empty on timeout).
        timed_out (bool): True iff the subprocess hit the per-step timeout.
    """

    step: CbmcStep
    command: str
    returncode: int
    stdout: str
    stderr: str
    timed_out: bool

    @property
    def succeeded(self) -> bool:
        """True iff the subprocess exited zero and did not time out."""
        return self.returncode == 0 and not self.timed_out


def main() -> None:
    """Run CBMC on a function."""
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description=(
            "Run CBMC on a function with loop unwinding = _CBMC_UNWIND, depth = _CBMC_DEPTH. "
            "Exits with status 0 on verification success."
        )
    )
    parser.add_argument("--function", required=True, help="Name of the function to verify.")
    parser.add_argument("--file", required=True, help="Path to the C file defining the function.")
    parser.add_argument(
        "-I",
        "--include-dir",
        action="append",
        default=[],
        dest="include_dirs",
        metavar="DIR",
        help="Directory to add to the include search path. May be repeated.",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()
    result = run_cbmc(
        function_to_verify=args.function,
        file_containing_function_to_verify=args.file,
        include_dirs=args.include_dirs,
    )
    print(result.response)
    sys.exit(result.returncode)


def run_cbmc(
    function_to_verify: str,
    file_containing_function_to_verify: str,
    include_dirs: list[str] | None = None,
    call_graph: CallGraph | None = None,
    cwd: str | None = None,
) -> RunCbmcResult:
    """Run CBMC on the given function with loop unwinding = 5, depth = 100.

    The pipeline is split into three logical steps — `goto-cc`, `goto-instrument`, and
    `cbmc` — each run as its own subprocess so that failures can be attributed to a
    specific step. Up to three pipeline attempts are made: an initial run, a retry
    when an apparent recursive-inlining error is detected, and a retry when a missing
    callee body is detected. The timeout is per CBMC subprocess attempt, not a total
    budget; a timeout in any single step terminates the whole call.

    Args:
        function_to_verify (str): Name of the function to verify.
        file_containing_function_to_verify (str): Path to the C file defining the function.
        include_dirs (list[str] | None): Directories to add to the C preprocessor's include
            search path. Forwarded to `goto-cc` as `-I` flags.
        call_graph (CallGraph | None): When provided, this pre-built call graph is used instead
            of parsing `file_containing_function_to_verify`.
        cwd (str | None): Working directory for every subprocess in the pipeline. The pipeline
            writes its intermediate `<function>.goto` / `checking-<function>-contracts.goto` files
            relative to this directory, so concurrent runs of the same function must each pass a
            distinct `cwd` to avoid clobbering one another. When None, subprocesses inherit the
            current working directory.

    Returns:
        RunCbmcResult: The outcome of the run, naming the failed step (if any) and carrying
            a printable response and the relevant exit code.
    """
    if call_graph is None:
        path_to_raw_call_graph = construct_call_graph(file_containing_function_to_verify)
        call_graph = CallGraph(json.loads(Path(path_to_raw_call_graph).read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
    callees = get_in_file_callees_for(function_to_verify, call_graph)
    # Building the stub index is inexpensive for now (there is a single file).
    # Re-visit this if/when we have more stub files to parse.
    stub_index = build_stub_index()
    stub_paths = get_stub_paths_for(function_to_verify, call_graph, stub_index)
    nondet_callees = get_unstubbed_external_callees_for(function_to_verify, call_graph, stub_index)

    step_records: list[dict] = []

    # Initial attempt.
    result, combined_stdout, combined_stderr = _run_pipeline(
        function_to_verify,
        callees,
        file_containing_function_to_verify,
        stub_paths=stub_paths,
        include_dirs=include_dirs,
        prevent_macro_expansion=False,
        step_records=step_records,
        cwd=cwd,
    )
    # First, check if the run was successful or if it timed out.
    if result.cbmc_ran_successfully or result.timed_out:
        _log_invocation(file_containing_function_to_verify, result, step_records, nondet_callees)
        return result

    # Recursion-inlining retry.
    if has_recursion_inlining_error_message(function_to_verify, combined_stdout, combined_stderr):
        callees = get_in_file_callees_for(
            function_to_verify,
            call_graph,
            include_self=call_graph.is_self_recursive(function_to_verify),
        )
        result, combined_stdout, combined_stderr = _run_pipeline(
            function_to_verify,
            callees,
            file_containing_function_to_verify,
            stub_paths=stub_paths,
            include_dirs=include_dirs,
            prevent_macro_expansion=False,
            step_records=step_records,
            cwd=cwd,
        )

    # Missing-body retry: re-run with macro expansion suppressed.
    if not result.cbmc_ran_successfully and has_missing_body_for_callee_or_function_message(
        combined_stdout, combined_stderr
    ):
        result, combined_stdout, combined_stderr = _run_pipeline(
            function_to_verify,
            callees,
            file_containing_function_to_verify,
            stub_paths=stub_paths,
            include_dirs=include_dirs,
            prevent_macro_expansion=True,
            step_records=step_records,
            cwd=cwd,
        )

    _log_invocation(file_containing_function_to_verify, result, step_records, nondet_callees)
    return result


def _run_pipeline(
    function_to_verify: str,
    callees: list[str],
    file_containing_function: str,
    stub_paths: list[str] | None,
    include_dirs: list[str] | None,
    prevent_macro_expansion: bool,
    step_records: list[dict],
    cwd: str | None = None,
) -> tuple[RunCbmcResult, str, str]:
    """Run the goto-cc → goto-instrument → cbmc pipeline once.

    Each subprocess is run separately so the first failure (or timeout) can be attributed
    to its logical step. Step records are appended to `step_records` for the JSONL log.

    Args:
        function_to_verify (str): The function under verification.
        callees (list[str]): Callees of the function, used by `--replace-call-with-contract`.
        file_containing_function (str): The C source file containing the function.
        stub_paths (list[str] | None): Extra `.c` stub files compiled in alongside the source.
        include_dirs (list[str] | None): Directories forwarded to `goto-cc` as `-I` flags.
        prevent_macro_expansion (bool): When True, disable macros CBMC can't model and inject
            the bundled C-library models before contract enforcement.
        step_records (list[dict]): Mutated in place — one record per subprocess invocation
            appended in order. Used by `_log_invocation` to produce the JSONL row.
        cwd (str | None): Working directory for every subprocess, forwarded to `_run_step`. The
            pipeline's intermediate `.goto` files are written relative to this directory.

    Returns:
        tuple[RunCbmcResult, str, str]: The result of the pipeline plus the concatenated
            stdout and stderr across every step that ran. The concatenated output is used by
            the retry triggers in `run_cbmc`.
    """
    commands: list[tuple[CbmcStep, str]] = [
        (
            CbmcStep.GOTO_CC,
            _get_goto_cc_command(
                function_to_verify,
                file_containing_function,
                stub_paths=stub_paths,
                include_dirs=include_dirs,
                prevent_macro_expansion=prevent_macro_expansion,
            ),
        ),
    ]
    if prevent_macro_expansion:
        commands.append(
            (
                CbmcStep.GOTO_INSTRUMENT,
                _get_goto_instrument_add_library_command(function_to_verify),
            )
        )
    commands.extend(
        [
            (
                CbmcStep.GOTO_INSTRUMENT,
                _get_goto_instrument_unwind_command(function_to_verify),
            ),
            (
                CbmcStep.GOTO_INSTRUMENT,
                _get_goto_instrument_contract_command(function_to_verify, callees),
            ),
            (CbmcStep.CBMC, _get_cbmc_check_command(function_to_verify)),
        ]
    )

    combined_stdout = ""
    combined_stderr = ""
    for step, command in commands:
        step_run = _run_step(step, command, cwd=cwd)
        step_records.append(
            {
                "step": step.value,
                "command": step_run.command,
                "returncode": step_run.returncode,
            }
        )
        combined_stdout += step_run.stdout
        combined_stderr += step_run.stderr
        if not step_run.succeeded:
            return (
                _result_from_failure(
                    function_to_verify, step_run, combined_stdout, combined_stderr
                ),
                combined_stdout,
                combined_stderr,
            )

    return (
        RunCbmcResult(
            function=function_to_verify,
            failed_step=None,
            timed_out=False,
            returncode=0,
            response=f"{function_to_verify} verified successfully",
        ),
        combined_stdout,
        combined_stderr,
    )


def _run_step(step: CbmcStep, command: str, cwd: str | None = None) -> _StepRun:
    """Run one pipeline step as a subprocess with the per-step timeout.

    Args:
        step (CbmcStep): The logical step this subprocess belongs to.
        command (str): The shell command to run.
        cwd (str | None): Working directory for the subprocess. When None, the subprocess
            inherits the current working directory. Distinct directories let concurrent
            pipelines for the same function avoid clobbering each other's `.goto` files.

    Returns:
        _StepRun: The captured outcome, including stdout/stderr or the timeout sentinel.
    """
    try:
        completed = subprocess.run(
            command,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            shell=True,
            check=False,
            timeout=_DEFAULT_RUN_CBMC_TIMEOUT_SEC,
            cwd=cwd,
        )
    except TimeoutExpired:
        return _StepRun(
            step=step,
            command=command,
            returncode=_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE,
            stdout="",
            stderr="",
            timed_out=True,
        )
    return _StepRun(
        step=step,
        command=command,
        returncode=completed.returncode,
        stdout=completed.stdout,
        stderr=completed.stderr,
        timed_out=False,
    )


def _result_from_failure(
    function: str, step_run: _StepRun, combined_stdout: str, combined_stderr: str
) -> RunCbmcResult:
    """Build a `RunCbmcResult` for a pipeline that failed at `step_run`.

    Args:
        function (str): The function under verification.
        step_run (_StepRun): The step that failed or timed out.
        combined_stdout (str): Concatenated stdout across every step that ran (including
            the failing one). Used to render the failure response.
        combined_stderr (str): Concatenated stderr across every step that ran.

    Returns:
        RunCbmcResult: A failure result whose `response` is a formatted, truncated block.
    """
    if step_run.timed_out:
        response = (
            f"Verification for '{function}' timed out after "
            f"{_DEFAULT_RUN_CBMC_TIMEOUT_SEC} second(s) during {step_run.step.value}"
        )
        return RunCbmcResult(
            function=function,
            failed_step=step_run.step,
            timed_out=True,
            returncode=_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE,
            response=response,
        )
    response = _format_failure_response(function, step_run.step, combined_stdout, combined_stderr)
    return RunCbmcResult(
        function=function,
        failed_step=step_run.step,
        timed_out=False,
        returncode=step_run.returncode,
        response=response,
    )


def has_recursion_inlining_error_message(function: str, stdout: str, stderr: str) -> bool:
    """Return True iff CBMC reports an error with inlining given function, which might be recursive.

    Args:
        function (str): The function that might be recursive.
        stdout (str): The stdout of a CBMC command.
        stderr (str): The stderr of a CBMC command.

    Returns:
        bool: True iff CBMC reports an error with inlining given function, which might be recursive.
    """
    recursion_error_message = f"Recursive call to '{function}' during inlining"
    return recursion_error_message in stdout or recursion_error_message in stderr


def has_missing_body_for_callee_or_function_message(stdout: str, stderr: str) -> bool:
    """Return True iff CBMC output indicates a callee or function body is missing.

    The CBMC error output contains the string "no body for callee" when a callee of a function under
    verification is missing its body.

    It contains the string "no body for function" in cases where a function body may be missing
    (e.g., when a stub file is not passed in).

    In this case, it doesn't hurt to re-run CBMC while suppressing
    macro expansion (e.g., `isspace` in ctype.h expands to `__ctype_loc`, which CBMC lacks a model
    for).

    Args:
        stdout (str): The stdout of a CBMC command.
        stderr (str): The stderr of a CBMC command.

    Returns:
        bool: True iff CBMC output indicates a callee body is missing.
    """
    missing_callee_indicator = "no body for callee"
    missing_function_indicator = "no body for function"
    return (
        missing_callee_indicator in stdout
        or missing_callee_indicator in stderr
        or missing_function_indicator in stdout
        or missing_function_indicator in stderr
    )


def _format_failure_response(function: str, failed_step: CbmcStep, stdout: str, stderr: str) -> str:
    """Format a CBMC failure response, leading with the FAILURE lines.

    After the header, the response always begins with the FAILURE lines extracted from
    stdout, so the verdict survives the harness's head-only inline preview: CBMC prints its
    FAILURE lines and verdict at the *tail* of stdout, which a positional truncation would
    otherwise bury. The full stderr and stdout follow; when the combined output exceeds
    `_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS`, each stream is replaced by its tail behind a truncation marker.
    For `goto-cc`/`goto-instrument` failures stdout has no FAILURE lines, so the response
    leads with stderr, where those tools print their diagnostics.

    Args:
        function (str): The name of the function that failed verification.
        failed_step (CbmcStep): The pipeline step that failed.
        stdout (str): The concatenated stdout across every step that ran.
        stderr (str): The concatenated stderr across every step that ran.

    Returns:
        str: The formatted CBMC failure response, leading with the FAILURE lines and
            truncated iff it has exceeded the char budget.
    """
    if failed_step is CbmcStep.CBMC:
        header = f"{function} failed to verify with the following errors:\n\n"
    else:
        header = f"{function} failed at {failed_step.value} with the following errors:\n\n"

    failure_section = _failure_lines_section(stdout)

    # Lead with the FAILURE lines, then show both streams in full when everything fits.
    full = f"{header}{failure_section}--- stderr ---\n{stderr}\n--- stdout ---\n{stdout}"
    if len(full) <= _MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS:
        return full

    # Over budget: keep the leading FAILURE lines and replace each stream with its tail.
    # The `_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS`-wide placeholder pads the marker's digit count so the
    # actual marker (with the real dropped count) cannot push us over budget.
    digit_pad = str(_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS)
    marker = f"[... {digit_pad} characters truncated ...]\n"
    fixed = (
        f"{header}{failure_section}--- stderr (tail) ---\n{marker}--- stdout (tail) ---\n{marker}"
    )
    remaining = max(_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS - len(fixed), 0)
    stdout_budget = int(remaining * _STDOUT_TAIL_SHARE)
    stderr_budget = remaining - stdout_budget

    stderr_section = _tail_section("stderr (tail)", stderr, stderr_budget)
    stdout_section = _tail_section("stdout (tail)", stdout, stdout_budget)

    response = f"{header}{failure_section}{stderr_section}{stdout_section}"
    # Hard clamp: the per-section budget accounting can drift by a few chars
    # against the `fixed` estimate, so guarantee we never exceed the cap.
    return response[:_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS]


def _failure_lines_section(stdout: str) -> str:
    """Render the leading "FAILURE lines" section of a failure response, or "" if none.

    Extracts the FAILURE lines from `stdout` and renders them as a labeled section, capped
    at half the total budget so a pathological run with tens of thousands of FAILURE lines
    can't blow past the limit on its own. Returns the empty string when stdout has no
    FAILURE lines (e.g. a goto-cc compile failure), so callers naturally lead with stderr.

    Args:
        stdout (str): The concatenated stdout across every step that ran.

    Returns:
        str: The labeled FAILURE-lines section ending in a newline, or "" if there are none.
    """
    failure_lines = [line for line in stdout.split("\n") if "FAILURE" in line]
    if not failure_lines:
        return ""
    failure_block = "\n".join(failure_lines)
    failure_cap = _MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS // 2
    if len(failure_block) > failure_cap:
        dropped = len(failure_block) - failure_cap
        failure_block = (
            f"[... {dropped} characters of FAILURE lines truncated ...]\n"
            f"{failure_block[-failure_cap:]}"
        )
    return f"--- FAILURE lines ---\n{failure_block}\n"


def _tail_section(label: str, content: str, budget: int) -> str:
    """Render a labeled section containing the tail of content within budget chars.

    Args:
        label (str): The label of the section.
        content (str): The content to truncate.
        budget (int): The maximum number of characters to include in the section.

    Returns:
        str: The labeled section containing the tail of content within budget chars.
    """
    if len(content) <= budget:
        body = content
    else:
        dropped = len(content) - budget
        body = f"[... {dropped} characters truncated ...]\n{content[-budget:]}"
    return f"--- {label} ---\n{body}\n"


def _log_invocation(
    file_under_verification: str,
    result: RunCbmcResult,
    step_records: list[dict],
    nondet_callees: list[str],
) -> None:
    """Log a CBMC invocation with the given arguments.

    Args:
        file_under_verification (str): The file that contains the function under verification.
        result (RunCbmcResult): The final outcome of the `run_cbmc` call.
        step_records (list[dict]): Per-subprocess records (one entry per step invocation across
            every pipeline attempt), each with keys `step`, `command`, `returncode`.
        nondet_callees (list[str]): The list of callees that CBMC treated as non-deterministic
            during verification.
    """
    source_path = Path(file_under_verification)
    log_path = source_path.with_name(f"{source_path.stem}-cbmc-runs.jsonl")
    record = {
        "ts": datetime.now(UTC).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
        "function": result.function,
        "file": file_under_verification,
        "failed_step": result.failed_step.value if result.failed_step is not None else None,
        "returncode": result.returncode,
        "timed_out": result.timed_out,
        "steps": step_records,
        "nondet_callees": nondet_callees,
    }
    try:
        with log_path.open("a") as f:
            f.write(json.dumps(record) + "\n")
    except OSError:
        # This does fail silently, but it shouldn't stop the tool from making progress.
        pass


def _get_goto_cc_command(
    function: str,
    file_containing_function: str,
    stub_paths: list[str] | None = None,
    include_dirs: list[str] | None = None,
    prevent_macro_expansion: bool = False,
) -> str:
    """Return the goto-cc command that compiles `file_containing_function` to a goto-binary.

    This is the first step of the CBMC pipeline, exposed as a standalone helper so
    callers can run only the compile phase — useful for cheaply detecting inputs that goto-cc
    rejects (e.g., a body-mutated source where mutating `p - q` to `p + q` is illegal C).

    Args:
        function (str): The function used as the goto-binary entry point.
        file_containing_function (str): Path to the C source file.
        stub_paths (list[str] | None): Extra `.c` stub files compiled in alongside the source.
        include_dirs (list[str] | None): Directories forwarded as `-I` flags.
        prevent_macro_expansion (bool): If True, disable macros that CBMC cannot model.

    Returns:
        str: A single shell command suitable for `subprocess.run(..., shell=True)`.
    """
    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
    flags_disabling_macro_expansion = (
        f"{' '.join(_DISABLE_MACRO_FLAGS)} " if prevent_macro_expansion else ""
    )
    extra_stub_args = f" {' '.join(shlex.quote(p) for p in stub_paths)}" if stub_paths else ""
    include_flags = "".join(f" -I {shlex.quote(d)}" for d in include_dirs) if include_dirs else ""
    return (
        f"goto-cc {flags_disabling_macro_expansion}-o {quoted_function}.goto"
        f"{include_flags} "
        f"{shlex.quote(file_containing_function)}{extra_stub_args} "
        f"--function {quoted_function}"
    )


def _get_goto_instrument_add_library_command(function: str) -> str:
    """Return the `goto-instrument --add-library` command for `function`'s goto-binary.

    This step injects CBMC's bundled C-library models (`isspace`, `strchr`, etc.) into
    the goto-binary before contract enforcement runs. It's only used when macro expansion
    is suppressed; without it, those calls are treated as nondeterministic, which loosens
    precision and can cause `goto-instrument` to emit "no body for function" warnings.

    Args:
        function (str): The function whose goto-binary should receive the library models.

    Returns:
        str: A single shell command.
    """
    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
    return f"goto-instrument --add-library {quoted_function}.goto {quoted_function}.goto"


def _get_goto_instrument_unwind_command(function: str) -> str:
    """Return the `goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind _CBMC_UNWIND` command for `function`.

    Args:
        function (str): The function whose goto-binary should be unwound.

    Returns:
        str: A single shell command.
    """
    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
    return (
        f"goto-instrument --partial-loops "
        f"--unwind {_CBMC_UNWIND} {quoted_function}.goto {quoted_function}.goto"
    )


def _get_goto_instrument_contract_command(function: str, callees: list[str]) -> str:
    """Return the `goto-instrument --enforce-contract` command for `function`.

    Args:
        function (str): The function whose contract should be enforced.
        callees (list[str]): Callees whose contracts should be substituted for the call sites
            via `--replace-call-with-contract`.

    Returns:
        str: A single shell command.
    """
    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
    replace_calls = "".join(f" --replace-call-with-contract {shlex.quote(c)}" for c in callees)
    return (
        f"goto-instrument{replace_calls} "
        f"--enforce-contract {quoted_function} "
        f"{quoted_function}.goto "
        f"checking-{quoted_function}-contracts.goto"
    )


def _get_cbmc_check_command(function: str) -> str:
    """Return the final `cbmc` command that checks `function`'s instrumented goto-binary.

    Args:
        function (str): The function under verification.

    Returns:
        str: A single shell command.
    """
    quoted_function = shlex.quote(function)
    return (
        f"cbmc checking-{quoted_function}-contracts.goto "
        f"--function {quoted_function} --depth {_CBMC_DEPTH}"
    )


def compile_with_goto_cc(
    function: str,
    file_path: str,
    include_dirs: list[str] | None = None,
) -> int:
    """Run only the goto-cc compile step on a C file and return its exit code.

    A zero exit code means goto-cc accepted the input; non-zero means it rejected it.

    Args:
        function (str): The function used as the goto-binary entry point.
        file_path (str): Path to the C file to compile.
        include_dirs (list[str] | None): Directories forwarded as `-I` flags.

    Returns:
        int: goto-cc's exit code (0 == compiled successfully).
    """
    compilation_command = _get_goto_cc_command(function, file_path, include_dirs=include_dirs)
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            compilation_command,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            shell=True,
            check=False,
            timeout=_GOTO_CC_TIMEOUT_SEC,
        )
    except TimeoutExpired:
        return _TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE
    return result.returncode


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

< size guarantees every point is matched by some i, so npoints reaches

__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
    size_t k;
    (k + 1 < nlz77points) ==> (lz77splitpoints[k] < lz77splitpoints[k + 1])
})
__CPROVER_requires(
    (nlz77points == 0) || (lz77splitpoints[nlz77points - 1] < lz77->size))
{
    size_t *splitpoints = 0;
    size_t npoints = 0;
    size_t i;
    /* The input is given as lz77 indices, but we want to see the uncompressed
    index values. */
    size_t pos = 0;
    if (nlz77points > 0)
    {
        for (i = 0; i < lz77->size; i++)
        {
            size_t length = lz77->dists[i] == 0 ? 1 : lz77->litlens[i];
            if (lz77splitpoints[npoints] == i)
            {
                ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(pos, &splitpoints, &npoints);
                if (npoints == nlz77points)
                    break;
            }
            pos += length;
        }
    }
    assert(npoints == nlz77points);

    fprintf(stderr, "block split points: ");
    for (i = 0; i < npoints; i++)
    {
        fprintf(stderr, "%d ", (int)splitpoints[i]);
    }
    fprintf(stderr, "(hex:");
    for (i = 0; i < npoints; i++)
    {
        fprintf(stderr, " %x", (int)splitpoints[i]);
    }
    fprintf(stderr, ")\n");

    free(splitpoints);
}

## What I added

A function contract (`/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c:5176`) with:

- **Memory-safety preconditions** — `lz77` and its store-sized `dists`/`litlens` arrays are fresh, and the `lz77splitpoints` array is fresh for `nlz77points` elements, with wrap-around guards on the byte-size computations.
- **Functional preconditions** — the split indices are strictly increasing (`lz77splitpoints[k-1] < lz77splitpoints[k]`) and the last one is `< lz77->size`. These guarantee that `npoints` only advances on a match while staying in `[0, nlz77points)`, that every split point is eventually matched, and that the closing `assert(npoints == nlz77points)` holds.

## Two issues worked through

1. **Overflow in the monotonicity quantifier.** My first draft wrote `(k + 1 < nlz77points) ==> lz77splitpoints[k] < lz77splitpoints[k+1]`. For `k == SIZE_MAX`, `k+1` wraps to `0`, making the guard true and triggering an out-of-bounds read at `lz77splitpoints[SIZE_MAX]`. Rewriting with `k-1` indexing (`0 < k && k < nlz77points`) keeps `k` bounded and avoids the wrap.

2. **The `goto-instrument` crash.** With the buggy precondition, the first pipeline attempt failed verification, which (because of the "no body for function 'malloc'" warnings) triggered the tool's `--add-library` retry — and `--enforce-contract` crashes on the malloc model's `should_malloc_fail` symbol (`malloc`/`realloc` reached inside the loop). Fixing the precondition so the **first** attempt passes sidesteps the retry entirely. No code was changed — only specifications were inserted.
